title: orchis mascula subsp. pinetorum
intro: Orchis mascula subsp. pinetorum, also known as the pine orchid, is a rare and endemic subspecies of orchid found in the mountainous regions of the Iberian Peninsula in Europe. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of pine orchid include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve circulation, and boost the immune system, making it a potential remedy for various health conditions. The herbal preparations of pine orchid typically involve drying and crushing the plant's roots and leaves, which can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures for medicinal purposes.
